rationale: In the theoretical framework, the citing paper uses Pocheptsova et al. (2009) to support a specific mechanism-level claim: depletion reduces effortful cognitive processing and increases reliance on intuition in consumer decisions. This is not a purely perfunctory literature-list mention; it helps justify why self-regulatory depletion is behaviorally consequential in consumer choice settings. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's exact context-effects design, the cited work meaningfully informs the conceptual setup that motivates the new delegation-based depletion argument.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Pocheptsova et al. (2009) as prior conceptual evidence that resource depletion changes how consumers process and decide. It uses that result as part of the theoretical foundation for proposing delegation as a new source of depletion and testing downstream self-control consequences.
